articles.headline: PM Unveils 'Yuva Shakti' Initiative on National Youth Day, Focus on AI and Green Skills
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister launches the 'Yuva Shakti' initiative, a comprehensive program aimed at empowering India's youth through advanced skill development and entrepreneurial support.
• The initiative emphasizes training in emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, and sustainable practices such as Green Hydrogen and Renewable Energy.
• A new national fund, 'Yuva Udyam Nidhi', has been established to provide seed capital and mentorship to young entrepreneurs.
• The program aims to create 5 million new job-ready professionals and support 1 million startups over the next five years.
• Integrates with existing schemes like Skill India Mission and Startup India, creating a synergistic ecosystem for youth development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Skill development
prelimsPoints: National Youth Day is celebrated on January 12th, marking Swami Vivekananda's birth anniversary.
The 'Yuva Shakti' initiative focuses on AI, ML, Data Science, Green Hydrogen, and Renewable Energy skills.
'Yuva Udyam Nidhi' is a new fund for seed capital and mentorship for young entrepreneurs.
Aims to create 5 million professionals and support 1 million startups in 5 years.
Integrates with existing schemes like Skill India Mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of youth-centric policies for India's demographic dividend.
Analyze the role of skill development in emerging technologies (AI, Green Tech) for economic growth and global competitiveness.
Evaluate the potential impact of such initiatives on reducing youth unemployment and fostering an entrepreneurial ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale skill development programs and measures for effective monitoring.
Highlight the convergence of government schemes to maximize impact on human resource development.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing a gathering of youth, with 'Yuva Shakti' banner in the background.
articles.trivia: Swami Vivekananda's original name was Narendranath Datta. He was a key figure in the introduction of the Indian philosophies of Vedanta and Yoga to the Western world.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Full Disclosure of Electoral Bond Data by ECI, Upholds Transparency
articles.explainer:
• In a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court has directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure complete transparency regarding electoral bonds.
• The court mandated the immediate publication of all data related to donors and recipient political parties, including bond numbers, on the ECI website.
• This decision comes in response to multiple petitions seeking greater transparency in political funding and challenging the anonymity provided by the electoral bond scheme.
• The ruling emphasizes the right to information of citizens regarding political funding, crucial for a healthy democracy.
• The ECI has been given a strict deadline to comply with the directive, with implications for upcoming state elections.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court rules on electoral bonds, mandating full disclosure by ECI.
ECI to publish all donor and recipient data, including bond numbers, on its website.
The ruling reinforces the citizen's right to information regarding political funding.
Electoral bonds were introduced in 2017 as a means of political funding.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on electoral bonds for transparency in political funding and Indian democracy.
Analyze the constitutional principles invoked by the court in balancing donor privacy with the public's right to information.
Critically evaluate the arguments for and against the electoral bond scheme in the context of electoral reforms.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding democratic values and ensuring accountability in governance.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: India and ASEAN Conclude Strategic Summit on Enhanced Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a special summit with ASEAN nations, focusing on bolstering maritime security and cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Key outcomes include agreements on joint naval exercises, enhanced information sharing, and capacity building for coastal surveillance.
• Discussions also covered collaborative efforts to combat piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking across shared maritime zones.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• A new 'Indo-ASEAN Maritime Dialogue Forum' has been established to facilitate ongoing strategic discussions and policy coordination.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India hosted a special summit with ASEAN nations on maritime security.
Agreements reached on joint naval exercises, information sharing, and coastal surveillance.
Focus areas include combating piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
The summit reinforces India's 'Act East' policy.
A new 'Indo-ASEAN Maritime Dialogue Forum' has been established.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of enhanced maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the context of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how India's 'Act East' policy contributes to regional stability and economic integration through such initiatives.
Examine the challenges posed by non-traditional security threats in the maritime domain and the collaborative solutions pursued by India and ASEAN.
Evaluate the potential impact of the 'Indo-ASEAN Maritime Dialogue Forum' on future regional security architecture.
Critically assess India's role as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from India and ASEAN nations shaking hands at a summit table.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand as its founding members.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial atmospheric re-entry test for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• This test marks a significant step towards India's ambition of developing cost-effective and sustainable space access.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) performed a controlled descent and landing simulation, validating key aerodynamic and guidance systems.
• Reusable launch vehicles are essential for reducing the cost of space missions by allowing the core components to be reused multiple times.
• This development places India among a select few nations working on advanced reusable space technology.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
The test involved an atmospheric re-entry and controlled descent simulation.
RLVs aim to reduce the cost of space missions through reusability.
RLV-TD stands for Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its potential impact on global space economy.
Analyze how indigenization in space technology contributes to India's self-reliance and national security.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with developing and deploying reusable launch systems.
Compare India's RLV efforts with those of other major spacefaring nations.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle during atmospheric re-entry.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on 19 April 1975 from Kapustin Yar, a Soviet rocket launch and development site.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Progress in Pilot Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report on the National Green Hydrogen Mission, highlighting significant advancements in pilot projects.
• Several large-scale green hydrogen production facilities have commenced operations, demonstrating viable electrolysis technologies and storage solutions.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Policy incentives for research, development, and deployment of green hydrogen infrastructure are attracting private investment.
• Challenges related to cost-effectiveness and scaling up production remain, but initial results are encouraging for India's energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission progress report released by MNRE.
Pilot projects for green hydrogen production are showing promising results.
Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and the policy measures required to overcome them.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen as a fuel for various sectors (transport, industry) and its role in India's energy transition.
Evaluate the environmental and economic benefits of transitioning to a hydrogen-based economy.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: RBI Announces Expansion of Digital Rupee Pilot Program to Tier-2 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced the expansion of its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot program, known as the 'Digital Rupee', to several Tier-2 cities across the country.
• This expansion aims to gather more diverse user feedback and test the scalability and interoperability of the digital currency in varied demographic and economic settings.
• The pilot program, initially launched in select cities, has shown promising results in retail and wholesale segments, focusing on transaction efficiency and security.
• The RBI is exploring various use cases for the Digital Rupee, including cross-border payments and programmable payments, in collaboration with commercial banks.
• A public awareness campaign will accompany the rollout to educate citizens about the benefits and features of the e-Rupee.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: RBI expands Digital Rupee (CBDC) pilot program to Tier-2 cities.
Aims to test scalability, interoperability, and gather user feedback.
Pilot covers both retail (e₹-R) and wholesale (e₹-W) segments.
Digital Rupee is a legal tender issued by the central bank.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and risks of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) like the Digital Rupee for the Indian economy and financial system.
Analyze the implications of CBDC expansion on financial inclusion, payment systems, and monetary policy effectiveness.
Examine the technological infrastructure and regulatory framework required for a successful nationwide rollout of the Digital Rupee.
Compare India's approach to CBDC with global trends and other countries' experiences.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a digital rupee symbol on a smartphone screen, representing digital payments.
articles.trivia: The first paper currency was introduced in India by the Bank of Hindustan (1770-1832).

articles.headline: Veteran Actress Jayashree Kabir Dies at 73
articles.explainer:
• Noted Indian actress Jayashree Kabir passed away at the age of 73.
• Known for her significant contributions to Bengali cinema, she starred in several critically acclaimed films.
• Her prominent works include 'Pratidwandi' (1970) by Satyajit Ray, 'Simana Periye' (1971), and 'Rupali Saikate' (1979).
• She was celebrated for her natural acting style and strong screen presence during the 1970s and 80s.
• Kabir left a lasting legacy in the cultural landscape of Indian cinema.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Jayashree Kabir was a veteran Indian actress.
Known for her work in Bengali cinema.
Starred in Satyajit Ray's 'Pratidwandi'.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of Jayashree Kabir from her younger days.
articles.trivia: Satyajit Ray is the only Indian to have received an Honorary Academy Award for Lifetime Achievement.

articles.headline: Noted Poet and Socialite Indira Devi Dhanrajgir Passes Away at 95
articles.explainer:
• Indira Devi Dhanrajgir, a prominent poet, socialite, and patron of arts, passed away at the age of 95.
• Hailing from the royal family of Dhanrajgir, she was known for her elegant literary contributions and philanthropic efforts.
• Her work often explored themes of heritage, spirituality, and human emotions, leaving a distinct mark on contemporary Indian poetry.
• She was also a well-known figure in Hyderabad's cultural circles, actively supporting various artistic and social causes.
• Her passing marks the end of an era for a generation of poets and cultural connoisseurs.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Indira Devi Dhanrajgir was a poet and socialite.
Known for contributions to Indian poetry and cultural patronage.
Associated with Hyderabad's cultural circles.
articles.imageDescription: A graceful portrait of Indira Devi Dhanrajgir.
articles.trivia: Hyderabad was historically known as a pearl and diamond trading centre, earning it the moniker 'City of Pearls'.

articles.headline: Former Assam MLA Bir Bhadra Hagjer Passes Away at 75
articles.explainer:
• Bir Bhadra Hagjer, a veteran politician and former Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Assam, passed away at the age of 75.
• He served as an MLA representing the Haflong constituency from 2016 to 2021.
• Hagjer was known for his dedication to public service and his efforts towards the development of the Dima Hasao district (formerly North Cachar Hills).
• His political career was marked by his advocacy for tribal rights and regional development in Assam.
• He was a respected figure across party lines for his integrity and commitment to his constituents.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Bir Bhadra Hagjer was a former MLA from Assam.
Represented the Haflong constituency (2016-2021).
Known for working for Dima Hasao district development and tribal rights.
articles.imageDescription: A formal photograph of Bir Bhadra Hagjer.
articles.trivia: The Assam Legislative Assembly is a unicameral legislature, currently consisting of 126 members.

articles.headline: Draft National Water Policy 2026 Emphasizes Integrated River Basin Management and Water Conservation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti has released the draft National Water Policy 2026 for public consultation, proposing a paradigm shift in water governance.
• Key provisions include integrated river basin management, promoting water-use efficiency, and prioritizing conservation and rejuvenation of water bodies.
• The policy advocates for a 'water-smart' agriculture system, encouraging less water-intensive crops and micro-irrigation techniques.
• It also addresses climate change impacts on water resources, proposing robust strategies for flood and drought management.
• The draft emphasizes community participation and data-driven decision-making for sustainable water resource management.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Jal Shakti released draft National Water Policy 2026.
Focuses on integrated river basin management and water-use efficiency.
Promotes 'water-smart' agriculture and micro-irrigation.
Addresses climate change impacts on water resources.
Emphasizes community participation in water management.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the salient features of the Draft National Water Policy 2026 and its potential to address India's growing water crisis.
Discuss the challenges in implementing integrated river basin management and the role of inter-state cooperation.
Examine how the policy's focus on 'water-smart' agriculture can contribute to both food security and water conservation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of community participation and data-driven approaches in achieving sustainable water resource management.
articles.imageDescription: A map of India overlaid with river systems, symbolizing integrated water management.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 18% of the world's population but only 4% of the world's renewable water resources.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Boosts Critical Infrastructure Along Ladakh Border
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of several key infrastructure projects, including strategic roads and bridges, along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) in Ladakh.
• These projects are crucial for enhancing connectivity, troop mobility, and logistics support for the Indian armed forces in challenging high-altitude terrains.
• The accelerated development is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen its border infrastructure in response to evolving geopolitical realities.
• Advanced engineering techniques and all-weather construction methodologies have been employed to ensure durability and year-round accessibility.
• The initiative also aims to boost socio-economic development in remote border areas by improving access to markets and services for local populations.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO completed strategic infrastructure projects along LAC in Ladakh.
Projects include roads and bridges, enhancing connectivity and troop mobility.
Part of India's strategy to strengthen border infrastructure.
BRO operates under the Ministry of Defence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing border infrastructure, particularly in regions like Ladakh, for India's national security and defense preparedness.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in constructing and maintaining infrastructure in high-altitude and harsh terrains.
Examine the socio-economic impact of improved border connectivity on the local populations and their integration with the mainstream economy.
Evaluate India's overall border management strategy in the context of geopolitical developments and regional security.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed road winding through rugged, snow-capped mountains in Ladakh.
articles.trivia: The world's highest motorable road, Umling La Pass, is located in Ladakh at an altitude of 19,024 feet (5,798 m) and was constructed by BRO.

articles.headline: Government Considers Expanding PLI Scheme to Plywood and Wooden Products Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government is reportedly considering expanding the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include the plywood and wooden products manufacturing sector.
• This move aims to boost domestic manufacturing, reduce imports, and create employment opportunities in a labor-intensive industry.
• The proposed PLI scheme would offer financial incentives to manufacturers achieving specified production and sales targets, encouraging investment in advanced technology and sustainable practices.
• The initiative seeks to make India a global manufacturing hub for high-quality wooden products, leveraging its vast forest resources (through sustainable sourcing).
• Stakeholders in the furniture and construction industries anticipate a significant positive impact on local supply chains and competitiveness.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Government considering expanding PLI scheme to plywood and wooden products.
Aims to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce imports.
PLI scheme provides incentives for achieving production and sales targets.
Focus on making India a manufacturing hub for wooden products.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind expanding the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to new sectors like plywood and wooden products.
Analyze the potential benefits of such an expansion for domestic manufacturing, employment generation, and import substitution.
Examine the challenges that the plywood and wooden products sector might face in leveraging the PLI scheme, including raw material sourcing and technological upgrades.
Evaluate the overall effectiveness of the PLI scheme in achieving India's 'Make in India' and economic growth objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Stacks of plywood sheets in a manufacturing facility.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme was first introduced in March 2020 for three sectors: mobile manufacturing and specified electronic components, critical Key Starting Materials (KSMs)/drug intermediates and Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), and manufacturing of medical devices.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Launches Major Restoration Project for Odisha's Ancient Temples
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has initiated a large-scale restoration and conservation project for a cluster of ancient temples in Odisha, particularly focusing on the Kalingan architectural style.
• The project aims to preserve the structural integrity and artistic heritage of these historically significant sites, many of which date back to the 7th-13th centuries CE.
• Advanced laser scanning, drone mapping, and traditional craftsmanship techniques are being employed for accurate restoration.
• This initiative is part of a broader national effort to protect and promote India's rich cultural heritage and boost heritage tourism.
• Local communities and cultural experts are being involved to ensure authenticity and sustained maintenance of the restored sites.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI launched a major restoration project for ancient temples in Odisha.
Focus on Kalingan architectural style temples (7th-13th centuries CE).
Utilizes advanced and traditional restoration techniques.
Part of efforts to protect cultural heritage and boost tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving ancient architectural heritage like the Kalingan temples of Odisha for India's cultural identity and tourism potential.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in conservation efforts and the role of modern technology in overcoming them.
Examine the balance between conservation and promoting tourism at historical sites, considering sustainable development principles.
Evaluate the importance of community involvement in the long-term preservation of cultural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: An intricately carved ancient temple in Odisha, showing ongoing restoration work.
articles.trivia: The Lingaraja Temple in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, is one of the finest examples of Kalingan architecture, dating back to the 11th century.

articles.headline: India Boosts Indigenous Vaccine Manufacturing with New Biopharma Hub
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Biotechnology announced the establishment of a new state-of-the-art biopharmaceutical manufacturing hub focused on indigenous vaccine production.
• This initiative aims to strengthen India's self-reliance in vaccine development and manufacturing, particularly for emerging infectious diseases and neglected tropical diseases.
• The hub will facilitate research, development, and large-scale production, integrating advanced genomic sequencing and rapid prototyping capabilities.
• It is expected to significantly reduce India's dependence on foreign imports for critical vaccines and enhance its role as a global vaccine supplier.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to leverage expertise and investment for accelerated development and deployment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: DBT announced a new biopharmaceutical manufacturing hub for indigenous vaccine production.
Aims to enhance India's self-reliance in vaccine development.
Focus on emerging infectious diseases and neglected tropical diseases.
Will integrate genomic sequencing and rapid prototyping.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of boosting indigenous vaccine manufacturing for India's public health security and its role as a global pharmaceutical hub.
Analyze how public-private partnerships can accelerate vaccine development and deployment, especially for neglected diseases.
Examine the challenges in establishing and sustaining a state-of-the-art biopharmaceutical manufacturing ecosystem in India.
Evaluate the lessons learned from recent pandemics in shaping India's current vaccine self-reliance strategy.
articles.imageDescription: A laboratory setting with scientists working on vaccine research and development.
articles.trivia: India is often referred to as the 'pharmacy of the world' due to its significant contribution to global generic drug and vaccine production.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Digital Learning Integration and Teacher Training
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting progress in digital learning integration.
• Significant strides have been made in developing digital educational content, establishing virtual labs, and expanding online learning platforms.
• A major focus is on comprehensive teacher training programs to equip educators with skills for blended learning environments and technology-aided pedagogy.
• The policy aims to bridge the digital divide in education, ensuring equitable access to quality learning resources across urban and rural areas.
• Challenges related to infrastructure, internet connectivity, and sustained teacher development remain key areas for ongoing intervention.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education provided an update on NEP 2020 implementation.
Focus on digital learning integration, virtual labs, and online platforms.
Emphasis on teacher training for blended learning.
Aims to bridge the digital divide in education.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of digital learning and technology in transforming education as envisioned by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating digital pedagogy, especially in diverse socio-economic contexts across India.
Examine the critical importance of teacher training and capacity building for the successful implementation of digital education initiatives.
Evaluate the NEP 2020's strategies for bridging the digital divide and ensuring equitable access to quality education.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom interacting with digital learning tools.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major overhaul of India's education system since independence, following policies in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India, during a high-level diplomatic engagement, reiterated its strong call for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• The Ministry of External Affairs emphasized the need for an expanded and more representative UNSC that reflects contemporary global realities, including permanent membership for India.
• India highlighted the outdated structure of the UNSC, arguing it no longer accurately represents the geopolitical landscape of the 21st century.
• The country stressed that a reformed UNSC is crucial for its legitimacy, effectiveness, and ability to address global challenges like peace, security, and climate change.
• India continues to garner support from various nations for its bid for permanent membership, advocating for equitable geographical representation.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India reiterated its call for UN Security Council reforms.
Demands expanded and more representative UNSC, including permanent seat for India.
Argues the current UNSC structure is outdated.
UNSC has 5 permanent members and 10 non-permanent members.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's long-standing demand for permanent membership in the UN Security Council and the rationale behind it.
Analyze the challenges and complexities involved in achieving comprehensive UNSC reforms, considering the interests of existing permanent members.
Examine the implications of an unreformed UNSC for global governance and its effectiveness in addressing contemporary international challenges.
Evaluate the arguments for and against expanding the permanent membership of the UNSC and the role India can play in a reformed council.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council is the only UN body with the authority to issue binding resolutions on member states.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase II Focuses on Tributary Restoration and Sustainable Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti provided an update on Phase II of the Namami Gange Mission, emphasizing the rejuvenation of tributaries and promoting sustainable livelihoods.
• Beyond cleaning the main stem of the Ganga, the new phase focuses on improving water quality and ecological health of rivers like Yamuna, Ramganga, and Kali.
• Community-based approaches are being scaled up to involve local populations in river conservation and waste management efforts.
• New initiatives include promoting organic farming along river banks, developing ecotourism, and supporting traditional livelihoods dependent on the river.
• The mission aims for a holistic approach to river basin management, integrating environmental protection with socio-economic development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Mission Phase II focuses on tributary rejuvenation.
Targets rivers like Yamuna, Ramganga, and Kali.
Emphasizes community-based approaches and sustainable livelihoods.
Promotes organic farming along river banks and ecotourism.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the shift in focus towards tributary restoration and sustainable livelihoods in Phase II of the Namami Gange Mission.
Discuss the challenges in achieving holistic river basin management, considering the multiple stakeholders and environmental complexities.
Analyze the potential of community participation and integrated approaches in enhancing the effectiveness and sustainability of river rejuvenation projects.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of promoting ecotourism and sustainable livelihoods along the Ganga and its tributaries.
articles.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the Ganga river with lush green banks, symbolizing rejuvenation efforts.
articles.trivia: The Ganga is India's longest river, flowing for over 2,525 kilometers.

articles.headline: Government Enhances E-Governance Initiatives for Improved Service Delivery in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new measures to strengthen e-governance initiatives in rural India.
• The focus is on expanding the reach of Common Service Centres (CSCs) and integrating more government services onto digital platforms.
• New digital literacy programs are being launched to empower rural citizens to access online services, including land records, health consultations, and financial aid applications.
• The goal is to improve transparency, reduce corruption, and enhance the efficiency of public service delivery at the grassroots level.
• Challenges related to internet penetration, digital infrastructure, and language barriers are being addressed through targeted interventions.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: MeitY enhances e-governance initiatives in rural India.
Focus on expanding Common Service Centres (CSCs) and digital platforms.
New digital literacy programs for rural citizens.
Aims to improve transparency and efficiency of public services.
Addresses challenges like internet penetration and language barriers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of e-governance initiatives in promoting inclusive development and improving public service delivery in rural India.
Analyze the role of Common Service Centres (CSCs) as a backbone for digital inclusion and access to government services at the grassroots level.
Examine the challenges faced in implementing e-governance in rural areas, including digital literacy, infrastructure, and ensuring cybersecurity.
Evaluate the impact of digital initiatives on transparency, accountability, and reducing corruption in governance.
articles.imageDescription: A rural Indian setting with a Common Service Centre sign, showing people accessing digital services.
articles.trivia: The National e-Governance Plan (NeGP) was launched in 2006 with the vision of 'Make all Government services accessible to the common man in his locality'.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Waters Treaty Mechanisms
articles.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held technical discussions regarding the implementation mechanisms of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in a constructive atmosphere.
• The talks focused on resolving specific issues related to the design and operation of certain hydroelectric projects on the western rivers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, which has governed water sharing between the two nations for over six decades.
• Expert-level delegations exchanged data and proposals, aiming to find mutually agreeable solutions without resorting to arbitration.
• The continuous dialogue under the Permanent Indus Commission is vital for maintaining peace and cooperation on shared water resources.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India and Pakistan held technical talks on Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
Discussions on design and operation of hydroelectric projects on western rivers.
Both nations committed to IWT provisions.
The Permanent Indus Commission facilitates dialogue under IWT.
IWT was signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a model for transboundary water cooperation and its role in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the complexities and challenges involved in implementing the IWT, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects on shared rivers.
Examine the importance of continued dialogue and technical discussions under the Permanent Indus Commission for resolving disputes peacefully.
Evaluate the potential impact of climate change and increasing water scarcity on the future of the Indus Waters Treaty.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus river system and its tributaries flowing through India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed by Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Ayub Khan in 1960.

highlights: PM unveils 'Yuva Shakti' Initiative on National Youth Day, focusing on AI and Green Skills for youth empowerment.
Supreme Court mandates full disclosure of Electoral Bond data by ECI, upholding transparency in political funding.
India and ASEAN conclude a strategic summit, agreeing on enhanced maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.
ISRO successfully conducts a critical test for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, advancing cost-effective space access.
Draft National Water Policy 2026 released, emphasizing integrated river basin management and 'water-smart' agriculture.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the 'Yuva Shakti' Initiative, recently launched by the Prime Minister, is/are correct?
1. It focuses on skill development in traditional crafts and agricultural practices.
2. It includes a national fund, 'Yuva Udyam Nidhi', to provide seed capital to young entrepreneurs.
3. The initiative aims to support 1 million startups over the next five years.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The 'Yuva Shakti' initiative emphasizes training in emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, Green Hydrogen, and Renewable Energy, not primarily traditional crafts. Statements 2 and 3 are correct as per the article on the 'Yuva Shakti' Initiative.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Electoral Bonds in India:
1. They were introduced as a means of political funding to enhance transparency.
2. The Supreme Court has recently directed the Election Commission of India to publish all data related to donors and recipient political parties.
3. Electoral bonds can be purchased by any Indian citizen or corporate entity.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect as while they were introduced as a means of political funding, the anonymity feature was criticized for reducing transparency, which the recent SC ruling addresses. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. The Supreme Court's ruling mandates full disclosure, and electoral bonds can indeed be purchased by citizens or corporates.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology being developed by ISRO, consider the following statements:
1. RLV technology aims to reduce the cost of space missions by allowing components to be reused.
2. ISRO has recently conducted an atmospheric re-entry test for its RLV Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
3. India is the first country globally to successfully demonstrate RLV technology.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct as per the article. Statement 3 is incorrect; several other nations, notably the USA with SpaceX's Falcon 9, have successfully demonstrated and utilized RLV technology prior to India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is/are the key focus areas of the Draft National Water Policy 2026?
1. Integrated river basin management.
2. Promotion of 'water-smart' agriculture.
3. Prioritizing only industrial water usage.
4. Community participation in water resource management.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 4 are explicitly mentioned as key focus areas in the Draft National Water Policy 2026. Statement 3 is incorrect; the policy emphasizes overall water-use efficiency and conservation, not prioritizing only industrial usage. It aims for a balanced and sustainable approach.

prelimsMocks.question: National Youth Day is celebrated in India on January 12th to commemorate the birth anniversary of which prominent personality?
prelimsMocks.explanation: National Youth Day is celebrated annually on January 12th to honor the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda, a great spiritual leader and philosopher.

mainsMocks.question: The launch of the 'Yuva Shakti' Initiative on National Youth Day signifies India's commitment to leveraging its demographic dividend. Discuss the multi-dimensional approach of this initiative and evaluate its potential impact on skill development, entrepreneurship, and inclusive economic growth.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Yuva Shakti Initiative and National Youth Day. Detail its multi-dimensional approach (AI, Green Skills, Yuva Udyam Nidhi, integration with existing schemes). Analyze the potential positive impacts on youth employment, startup ecosystem, and economic growth. Discuss challenges in implementation and suggest measures for success. Conclude on the importance of human capital for India's future.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's directive for full disclosure of electoral bond data is a significant step towards transparency in political funding. Analyze the implications of this judgment for Indian democracy, focusing on the balance between donor privacy and the public's right to information. What further electoral reforms are needed to ensure a truly transparent political financing system?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions. Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the context of the SC ruling on electoral bonds and its significance. Explain the concept of electoral bonds and the debate around transparency vs. anonymity. Discuss the implications for democracy (informed electorate, accountability, level playing field). Analyze the constitutional basis for the right to information. Propose other necessary electoral reforms (e.g., state funding, cap on individual donations, stronger ECI powers). Conclude on the ongoing evolution of electoral transparency in India.

mainsMocks.question: India's enhanced maritime security cooperation with ASEAN, as seen in the recent summit, is crucial for stability and growth in the Indo-Pacific. Discuss the strategic rationale behind this collaboration and examine how it addresses both traditional and non-traditional security threats in the region.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations. Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the India-ASEAN summit on maritime security and its context in the Indo-Pacific. Explain the strategic rationale for cooperation (Act East Policy, shared interests, countering challenges). Detail how collaboration addresses traditional threats (naval exercises, piracy) and non-traditional threats (illegal fishing, drug trafficking, environmental concerns). Discuss the broader geopolitical implications for regional stability and India's role. Conclude on the importance of multilateral cooperation for a free and open Indo-Pacific.

quickBytes.text: National Youth Day is celebrated on January 12th, the birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda.
The 'Yuva Shakti' Initiative focuses on AI, ML, Data Science, and Green Hydrogen skills.
Electoral Bonds were introduced in 2017 to facilitate political funding.
ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was founded in 1967.
ISRO's RLV-TD is a step towards developing cost-effective and sustainable space access.
Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The Digital Rupee (e₹) is India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot program.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) constructs and maintains road networks in border areas.
Kalingan architectural style is prominent in temples of Odisha, such as Lingaraja Temple.
The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960.

GS1.headline: Veteran Actress Jayashree Kabir Dies at 73
GS1.explainer:
• Noted Indian actress Jayashree Kabir passed away at the age of 73.
• Known for her significant contributions to Bengali cinema, she starred in several critically acclaimed films.
• Her prominent works include 'Pratidwandi' (1970) by Satyajit Ray, 'Simana Periye' (1971), and 'Rupali Saikate' (1979).
• She was celebrated for her natural acting style and strong screen presence during the 1970s and 80s.
• Kabir left a lasting legacy in the cultural landscape of Indian cinema.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Jayashree Kabir was a veteran Indian actress.
Known for her work in Bengali cinema.
Starred in Satyajit Ray's 'Pratidwandi'.
GS1.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of Jayashree Kabir from her younger days.
GS1.trivia: Satyajit Ray is the only Indian to have received an Honorary Academy Award for Lifetime Achievement.

GS1.headline: Noted Poet and Socialite Indira Devi Dhanrajgir Passes Away at 95
GS1.explainer:
• Indira Devi Dhanrajgir, a prominent poet, socialite, and patron of arts, passed away at the age of 95.
• Hailing from the royal family of Dhanrajgir, she was known for her elegant literary contributions and philanthropic efforts.
• Her work often explored themes of heritage, spirituality, and human emotions, leaving a distinct mark on contemporary Indian poetry.
• She was also a well-known figure in Hyderabad's cultural circles, actively supporting various artistic and social causes.
• Her passing marks the end of an era for a generation of poets and cultural connoisseurs.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Indira Devi Dhanrajgir was a poet and socialite.
Known for contributions to Indian poetry and cultural patronage.
Associated with Hyderabad's cultural circles.
GS1.imageDescription: A graceful portrait of Indira Devi Dhanrajgir.
GS1.trivia: Hyderabad was historically known as a pearl and diamond trading centre, earning it the moniker 'City of Pearls'.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Launches Major Restoration Project for Odisha's Ancient Temples
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has initiated a large-scale restoration and conservation project for a cluster of ancient temples in Odisha, particularly focusing on the Kalingan architectural style.
• The project aims to preserve the structural integrity and artistic heritage of these historically significant sites, many of which date back to the 7th-13th centuries CE.
• Advanced laser scanning, drone mapping, and traditional craftsmanship techniques are being employed for accurate restoration.
• This initiative is part of a broader national effort to protect and promote India's rich cultural heritage and boost heritage tourism.
• Local communities and cultural experts are being involved to ensure authenticity and sustained maintenance of the restored sites.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI launched a major restoration project for ancient temples in Odisha.
Focus on Kalingan architectural style temples (7th-13th centuries CE).
Utilizes advanced and traditional restoration techniques.
Part of efforts to protect cultural heritage and boost tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving ancient architectural heritage like the Kalingan temples of Odisha for India's cultural identity and tourism potential.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in conservation efforts and the role of modern technology in overcoming them.
Examine the balance between conservation and promoting tourism at historical sites, considering sustainable development principles.
Evaluate the importance of community involvement in the long-term preservation of cultural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: An intricately carved ancient temple in Odisha, showing ongoing restoration work.
GS1.trivia: The Lingaraja Temple in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, is one of the finest examples of Kalingan architecture, dating back to the 11th century.

GS2.headline: PM Unveils 'Yuva Shakti' Initiative on National Youth Day, Focus on AI and Green Skills
GS2.explainer:
• Prime Minister launches the 'Yuva Shakti' initiative, a comprehensive program aimed at empowering India's youth through advanced skill development and entrepreneurial support.
• The initiative emphasizes training in emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, and sustainable practices such as Green Hydrogen and Renewable Energy.
• A new national fund, 'Yuva Udyam Nidhi', has been established to provide seed capital and mentorship to young entrepreneurs.
• The program aims to create 5 million new job-ready professionals and support 1 million startups over the next five years.
• Integrates with existing schemes like Skill India Mission and Startup India, creating a synergistic ecosystem for youth development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Skill development
prelimsPoints: National Youth Day is celebrated on January 12th, marking Swami Vivekananda's birth anniversary.
The 'Yuva Shakti' initiative focuses on AI, ML, Data Science, Green Hydrogen, and Renewable Energy skills.
'Yuva Udyam Nidhi' is a new fund for seed capital and mentorship for young entrepreneurs.
Aims to create 5 million professionals and support 1 million startups in 5 years.
Integrates with existing schemes like Skill India Mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of youth-centric policies for India's demographic dividend.
Analyze the role of skill development in emerging technologies (AI, Green Tech) for economic growth and global competitiveness.
Evaluate the potential impact of such initiatives on reducing youth unemployment and fostering an entrepreneurial ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale skill development programs and measures for effective monitoring.
Highlight the convergence of government schemes to maximize impact on human resource development.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing a gathering of youth, with 'Yuva Shakti' banner in the background.
GS2.trivia: Swami Vivekananda's original name was Narendranath Datta. He was a key figure in the introduction of the Indian philosophies of Vedanta and Yoga to the Western world.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Full Disclosure of Electoral Bond Data by ECI, Upholds Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• In a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court has directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure complete transparency regarding electoral bonds.
• The court mandated the immediate publication of all data related to donors and recipient political parties, including bond numbers, on the ECI website.
• This decision comes in response to multiple petitions seeking greater transparency in political funding and challenging the anonymity provided by the electoral bond scheme.
• The ruling emphasizes the right to information of citizens regarding political funding, crucial for a healthy democracy.
• The ECI has been given a strict deadline to comply with the directive, with implications for upcoming state elections.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court rules on electoral bonds, mandating full disclosure by ECI.
ECI to publish all donor and recipient data, including bond numbers, on its website.
The ruling reinforces the citizen's right to information regarding political funding.
Electoral bonds were introduced in 2017 as a means of political funding.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on electoral bonds for transparency in political funding and Indian democracy.
Analyze the constitutional principles invoked by the court in balancing donor privacy with the public's right to information.
Critically evaluate the arguments for and against the electoral bond scheme in the context of electoral reforms.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding democratic values and ensuring accountability in governance.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

GS2.headline: India and ASEAN Conclude Strategic Summit on Enhanced Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a special summit with ASEAN nations, focusing on bolstering maritime security and cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Key outcomes include agreements on joint naval exercises, enhanced information sharing, and capacity building for coastal surveillance.
• Discussions also covered collaborative efforts to combat piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking across shared maritime zones.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• A new 'Indo-ASEAN Maritime Dialogue Forum' has been established to facilitate ongoing strategic discussions and policy coordination.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India hosted a special summit with ASEAN nations on maritime security.
Agreements reached on joint naval exercises, information sharing, and coastal surveillance.
Focus areas include combating piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
The summit reinforces India's 'Act East' policy.
A new 'Indo-ASEAN Maritime Dialogue Forum' has been established.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of enhanced maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the context of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how India's 'Act East' policy contributes to regional stability and economic integration through such initiatives.
Examine the challenges posed by non-traditional security threats in the maritime domain and the collaborative solutions pursued by India and ASEAN.
Evaluate the potential impact of the 'Indo-ASEAN Maritime Dialogue Forum' on future regional security architecture.
Critically assess India's role as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from India and ASEAN nations shaking hands at a summit table.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand as its founding members.

GS2.headline: Former Assam MLA Bir Bhadra Hagjer Passes Away at 75
GS2.explainer:
• Bir Bhadra Hagjer, a veteran politician and former Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Assam, passed away at the age of 75.
• He served as an MLA representing the Haflong constituency from 2016 to 2021.
• Hagjer was known for his dedication to public service and his efforts towards the development of the Dima Hasao district (formerly North Cachar Hills).
• His political career was marked by his advocacy for tribal rights and regional development in Assam.
• He was a respected figure across party lines for his integrity and commitment to his constituents.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Bir Bhadra Hagjer was a former MLA from Assam.
Represented the Haflong constituency (2016-2021).
Known for working for Dima Hasao district development and tribal rights.
GS2.imageDescription: A formal photograph of Bir Bhadra Hagjer.
GS2.trivia: The Assam Legislative Assembly is a unicameral legislature, currently consisting of 126 members.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Digital Learning Integration and Teacher Training
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting progress in digital learning integration.
• Significant strides have been made in developing digital educational content, establishing virtual labs, and expanding online learning platforms.
• A major focus is on comprehensive teacher training programs to equip educators with skills for blended learning environments and technology-aided pedagogy.
• The policy aims to bridge the digital divide in education, ensuring equitable access to quality learning resources across urban and rural areas.
• Challenges related to infrastructure, internet connectivity, and sustained teacher development remain key areas for ongoing intervention.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education provided an update on NEP 2020 implementation.
Focus on digital learning integration, virtual labs, and online platforms.
Emphasis on teacher training for blended learning.
Aims to bridge the digital divide in education.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of digital learning and technology in transforming education as envisioned by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating digital pedagogy, especially in diverse socio-economic contexts across India.
Examine the critical importance of teacher training and capacity building for the successful implementation of digital education initiatives.
Evaluate the NEP 2020's strategies for bridging the digital divide and ensuring equitable access to quality education.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom interacting with digital learning tools.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major overhaul of India's education system since independence, following policies in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• India, during a high-level diplomatic engagement, reiterated its strong call for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• The Ministry of External Affairs emphasized the need for an expanded and more representative UNSC that reflects contemporary global realities, including permanent membership for India.
• India highlighted the outdated structure of the UNSC, arguing it no longer accurately represents the geopolitical landscape of the 21st century.
• The country stressed that a reformed UNSC is crucial for its legitimacy, effectiveness, and ability to address global challenges like peace, security, and climate change.
• India continues to garner support from various nations for its bid for permanent membership, advocating for equitable geographical representation.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India reiterated its call for UN Security Council reforms.
Demands expanded and more representative UNSC, including permanent seat for India.
Argues the current UNSC structure is outdated.
UNSC has 5 permanent members and 10 non-permanent members.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's long-standing demand for permanent membership in the UN Security Council and the rationale behind it.
Analyze the challenges and complexities involved in achieving comprehensive UNSC reforms, considering the interests of existing permanent members.
Examine the implications of an unreformed UNSC for global governance and its effectiveness in addressing contemporary international challenges.
Evaluate the arguments for and against expanding the permanent membership of the UNSC and the role India can play in a reformed council.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council is the only UN body with the authority to issue binding resolutions on member states.

GS2.headline: Government Enhances E-Governance Initiatives for Improved Service Delivery in Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new measures to strengthen e-governance initiatives in rural India.
• The focus is on expanding the reach of Common Service Centres (CSCs) and integrating more government services onto digital platforms.
• New digital literacy programs are being launched to empower rural citizens to access online services, including land records, health consultations, and financial aid applications.
• The goal is to improve transparency, reduce corruption, and enhance the efficiency of public service delivery at the grassroots level.
• Challenges related to internet penetration, digital infrastructure, and language barriers are being addressed through targeted interventions.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: MeitY enhances e-governance initiatives in rural India.
Focus on expanding Common Service Centres (CSCs) and digital platforms.
New digital literacy programs for rural citizens.
Aims to improve transparency and efficiency of public services.
Addresses challenges like internet penetration and language barriers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of e-governance initiatives in promoting inclusive development and improving public service delivery in rural India.
Analyze the role of Common Service Centres (CSCs) as a backbone for digital inclusion and access to government services at the grassroots level.
Examine the challenges faced in implementing e-governance in rural areas, including digital literacy, infrastructure, and ensuring cybersecurity.
Evaluate the impact of digital initiatives on transparency, accountability, and reducing corruption in governance.
GS2.imageDescription: A rural Indian setting with a Common Service Centre sign, showing people accessing digital services.
GS2.trivia: The National e-Governance Plan (NeGP) was launched in 2006 with the vision of 'Make all Government services accessible to the common man in his locality'.

GS2.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Waters Treaty Mechanisms
GS2.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held technical discussions regarding the implementation mechanisms of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in a constructive atmosphere.
• The talks focused on resolving specific issues related to the design and operation of certain hydroelectric projects on the western rivers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, which has governed water sharing between the two nations for over six decades.
• Expert-level delegations exchanged data and proposals, aiming to find mutually agreeable solutions without resorting to arbitration.
• The continuous dialogue under the Permanent Indus Commission is vital for maintaining peace and cooperation on shared water resources.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India and Pakistan held technical talks on Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
Discussions on design and operation of hydroelectric projects on western rivers.
Both nations committed to IWT provisions.
The Permanent Indus Commission facilitates dialogue under IWT.
IWT was signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a model for transboundary water cooperation and its role in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the complexities and challenges involved in implementing the IWT, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects on shared rivers.
Examine the importance of continued dialogue and technical discussions under the Permanent Indus Commission for resolving disputes peacefully.
Evaluate the potential impact of climate change and increasing water scarcity on the future of the Indus Waters Treaty.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus river system and its tributaries flowing through India and Pakistan.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed by Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Ayub Khan in 1960.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial atmospheric re-entry test for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• This test marks a significant step towards India's ambition of developing cost-effective and sustainable space access.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) performed a controlled descent and landing simulation, validating key aerodynamic and guidance systems.
• Reusable launch vehicles are essential for reducing the cost of space missions by allowing the core components to be reused multiple times.
• This development places India among a select few nations working on advanced reusable space technology.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
The test involved an atmospheric re-entry and controlled descent simulation.
RLVs aim to reduce the cost of space missions through reusability.
RLV-TD stands for Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its potential impact on global space economy.
Analyze how indigenization in space technology contributes to India's self-reliance and national security.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with developing and deploying reusable launch systems.
Compare India's RLV efforts with those of other major spacefaring nations.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle during atmospheric re-entry.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on 19 April 1975 from Kapustin Yar, a Soviet rocket launch and development site.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Progress in Pilot Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report on the National Green Hydrogen Mission, highlighting significant advancements in pilot projects.
• Several large-scale green hydrogen production facilities have commenced operations, demonstrating viable electrolysis technologies and storage solutions.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Policy incentives for research, development, and deployment of green hydrogen infrastructure are attracting private investment.
• Challenges related to cost-effectiveness and scaling up production remain, but initial results are encouraging for India's energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission progress report released by MNRE.
Pilot projects for green hydrogen production are showing promising results.
Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and the policy measures required to overcome them.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen as a fuel for various sectors (transport, industry) and its role in India's energy transition.
Evaluate the environmental and economic benefits of transitioning to a hydrogen-based economy.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Announces Expansion of Digital Rupee Pilot Program to Tier-2 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced the expansion of its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot program, known as the 'Digital Rupee', to several Tier-2 cities across the country.
• This expansion aims to gather more diverse user feedback and test the scalability and interoperability of the digital currency in varied demographic and economic settings.
• The pilot program, initially launched in select cities, has shown promising results in retail and wholesale segments, focusing on transaction efficiency and security.
• The RBI is exploring various use cases for the Digital Rupee, including cross-border payments and programmable payments, in collaboration with commercial banks.
• A public awareness campaign will accompany the rollout to educate citizens about the benefits and features of the e-Rupee.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: RBI expands Digital Rupee (CBDC) pilot program to Tier-2 cities.
Aims to test scalability, interoperability, and gather user feedback.
Pilot covers both retail (e₹-R) and wholesale (e₹-W) segments.
Digital Rupee is a legal tender issued by the central bank.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and risks of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) like the Digital Rupee for the Indian economy and financial system.
Analyze the implications of CBDC expansion on financial inclusion, payment systems, and monetary policy effectiveness.
Examine the technological infrastructure and regulatory framework required for a successful nationwide rollout of the Digital Rupee.
Compare India's approach to CBDC with global trends and other countries' experiences.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of a digital rupee symbol on a smartphone screen, representing digital payments.
GS3.trivia: The first paper currency was introduced in India by the Bank of Hindustan (1770-1832).

GS3.headline: Draft National Water Policy 2026 Emphasizes Integrated River Basin Management and Water Conservation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti has released the draft National Water Policy 2026 for public consultation, proposing a paradigm shift in water governance.
• Key provisions include integrated river basin management, promoting water-use efficiency, and prioritizing conservation and rejuvenation of water bodies.
• The policy advocates for a 'water-smart' agriculture system, encouraging less water-intensive crops and micro-irrigation techniques.
• It also addresses climate change impacts on water resources, proposing robust strategies for flood and drought management.
• The draft emphasizes community participation and data-driven decision-making for sustainable water resource management.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Jal Shakti released draft National Water Policy 2026.
Focuses on integrated river basin management and water-use efficiency.
Promotes 'water-smart' agriculture and micro-irrigation.
Addresses climate change impacts on water resources.
Emphasizes community participation in water management.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the salient features of the Draft National Water Policy 2026 and its potential to address India's growing water crisis.
Discuss the challenges in implementing integrated river basin management and the role of inter-state cooperation.
Examine how the policy's focus on 'water-smart' agriculture can contribute to both food security and water conservation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of community participation and data-driven approaches in achieving sustainable water resource management.
GS3.imageDescription: A map of India overlaid with river systems, symbolizing integrated water management.
GS3.trivia: India accounts for about 18% of the world's population but only 4% of the world's renewable water resources.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Boosts Critical Infrastructure Along Ladakh Border
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of several key infrastructure projects, including strategic roads and bridges, along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) in Ladakh.
• These projects are crucial for enhancing connectivity, troop mobility, and logistics support for the Indian armed forces in challenging high-altitude terrains.
• The accelerated development is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen its border infrastructure in response to evolving geopolitical realities.
• Advanced engineering techniques and all-weather construction methodologies have been employed to ensure durability and year-round accessibility.
• The initiative also aims to boost socio-economic development in remote border areas by improving access to markets and services for local populations.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO completed strategic infrastructure projects along LAC in Ladakh.
Projects include roads and bridges, enhancing connectivity and troop mobility.
Part of India's strategy to strengthen border infrastructure.
BRO operates under the Ministry of Defence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing border infrastructure, particularly in regions like Ladakh, for India's national security and defense preparedness.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in constructing and maintaining infrastructure in high-altitude and harsh terrains.
Examine the socio-economic impact of improved border connectivity on the local populations and their integration with the mainstream economy.
Evaluate India's overall border management strategy in the context of geopolitical developments and regional security.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed road winding through rugged, snow-capped mountains in Ladakh.
GS3.trivia: The world's highest motorable road, Umling La Pass, is located in Ladakh at an altitude of 19,024 feet (5,798 m) and was constructed by BRO.

GS3.headline: Government Considers Expanding PLI Scheme to Plywood and Wooden Products Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government is reportedly considering expanding the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include the plywood and wooden products manufacturing sector.
• This move aims to boost domestic manufacturing, reduce imports, and create employment opportunities in a labor-intensive industry.
